reasoning: For the resolution criteria to be met, Spain would need to record strictly greater than 10 (i.e., 11 or more) "Explosions/Remote violence" events in the 30 days preceding March 22, 2026. The most recent data from ACLED for Spain indicates a baseline value of exactly 1.000, meaning there were 0 explosions per 30-day interval over the preceding year. A web search for recent news in Spain covering February and March 2026 returns zero reports of any bombings, airstrikes, artillery fire, or mass remote violence. Given the stability of Spain, the absence of any ongoing armed conflict, and the fact that 11+ remote violence incidents would unequivocally dominate international news if they had happened, the probability of 11+ unrecorded or incoming events in the remainder of the period is negligible. I estimate the likelihood at the minimum floor threshold of 0.02.
